conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution independent of MW for identical chemical functionality
conditions.evidence_text: The critical solvent composition for PIB was established as THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w). ... According to Fig. 6 in THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w) PIB samples with identical chemical functionality eluted at the same position, independently of their MW.
conditions.notes: Used for determination of critical conditions. ELS detector used for analysis in Fig 6-8.
conditions.physical_address: Polymer Science Program, Department of Chemistry, University of Massachusetts Lowell, One University Avenue, Lowell, Massachusetts 01854, USA

conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution independent of MW for identical chemical functionality
conditions.evidence_text: Fig. 9 depicts LCCC chromatograms of PIB samples of different functionality but similar MW (approximately 2000) under critical condition using RI detector as the concentration detector. ... The LCCC chromatogram of PIBOL014 revealed three peaks (Fig. 11) with elution times correlating to PIB-diol, PIB-monool and PIB without OH functionality.
conditions.notes: Used for quantitative analysis of chemical heterogeneity. RI detector identified as better concentration detector than ELS.
conditions.physical_address: Polymer Science Program, Department of Chemistry, University of Massachusetts Lowell, One University Avenue, Lowell, Massachusetts 01854, USA
